questions.q:
a) What are the different isotopes of hydrogen and their masses? Describe the preparation of each isotope.
b) What are the uses of hydrogen isotope tritium?
questions.answer:
a) Hydrogen has three naturally occurring isotopes: Protium (1H), Deuterium (2H or D), and Tritium (3H or T).
Protium has a mass of 1 u and contains one proton and no neutrons in its atomic nucleus.
Deuterium has a mass of 2 u and contains one proton and one neutron in its atomic nucleus. It can be prepared by the electrolysis of heavy water (D2O), which is obtained from natural water by distillation.
Tritium has a mass of 3 u and contains one proton and two neutrons in its atomic nucleus. It is prepared by the reaction of lithium-6 with tritium gas, which is obtained by the irradiation of lithium-6 with neutrons.
b) Tritium has several uses, including as a fuel in nuclear fusion reactions, as a source of beta radiation in radiotherapy, and as a tracer in chemical reactions.
marking_scheme: 1 mark for listing the isotopes and their masses
1 mark for describing the preparation of Protium and Deuterium
1 mark for describing the preparation of Tritium
1 mark for listing the uses of tritium
1 mark for elaborating on the uses of tritium

questions.q: What are the physical and chemical properties of hydrogen? Describe the preparation and properties of hydrogen gas.
questions.topic: Physical and Chemical Properties of Hydrogen
questions.answer:
Hydrogen is a colorless, odorless, tasteless, and highly flammable gas. It is the lightest and most abundant element in the universe.
Physical properties of hydrogen include its melting point (14 K), boiling point (20 K), and density (0.0899 g/L).
Chemical properties of hydrogen include its ability to form compounds with almost all other elements, its high reactivity, and its ability to form hydrogen bonds.
Hydrogen gas can be prepared by the reaction of zinc with hydrochloric acid, the electrolysis of water, or the reaction of calcium carbide with water.
Hydrogen gas is highly flammable and can be ignited by a spark or flame. It is also highly reactive and can react with many metals to form hydrides.
marking_scheme: 1 mark for describing the physical properties of hydrogen
1 mark for describing the chemical properties of hydrogen
1 mark for describing the preparation of hydrogen gas
1 mark for describing the properties of hydrogen gas
1 mark for elaborating on the reactivity of hydrogen gas

questions.q: Describe the combustion reaction of hydrogen with oxygen to form water. What are the products of this reaction in the presence of a catalyst and without a catalyst?
questions.answer:
The combustion reaction of hydrogen with oxygen to form water is a highly exothermic reaction.
The balanced equation for this reaction is: 2H2 + O2 → 2H2O.
In the presence of a catalyst, such as platinum or palladium, the reaction is faster and more efficient.
Without a catalyst, the reaction is slower and requires a higher temperature and pressure.
The products of this reaction are always water and heat, regardless of the presence or absence of a catalyst.
marking_scheme: 1 mark for describing the balanced equation of the reaction
1 mark for describing the role of a catalyst in the reaction
1 mark for describing the products of the reaction in the presence of a catalyst
1 mark for describing the products of the reaction without a catalyst
1 mark for elaborating on the importance of catalysts in chemical reactions

questions.q: Describe the preparation and properties of hydrogen peroxide. What are the uses of hydrogen peroxide?
questions.answer:
Hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) is a colorless, syrupy liquid with a characteristic pungent odor. It is a strong oxidizing agent and can be prepared by the reaction of hydrogen with oxygen in the presence of a catalyst, such as manganese dioxide.
Hydrogen peroxide has several properties, including its ability to act as a bleach, its use as a disinfectant, and its ability to oxidize many substances.
Hydrogen peroxide has several uses, including as a disinfectant in medical and dental applications, as a bleach in laundry and cleaning, and as a propellant in rocket engines.
marking_scheme: 1 mark for describing the preparation of hydrogen peroxide
1 mark for describing the properties of hydrogen peroxide
1 mark for listing the uses of hydrogen peroxide
1 mark for elaborating on the use of hydrogen peroxide as a disinfectant
1 mark for elaborating on the use of hydrogen peroxide as a bleach

questions.q: What is the structure of the hydrogen molecule (H2)? Describe the bonding in the hydrogen molecule. What is the shape of the hydrogen molecule?
questions.answer:
The hydrogen molecule (H2) consists of two hydrogen atoms bonded together by a covalent bond.
The structure of the hydrogen molecule is as follows: H—H.
The bonding in the hydrogen molecule is a sigma (σ) bond, which is formed by the overlap of the 1s atomic orbitals of the two hydrogen atoms.
The shape of the hydrogen molecule is linear, with the two hydrogen atoms bonded together in a straight line.
marking_scheme: 1 mark for describing the structure of the hydrogen molecule
1 mark for describing the bonding in the hydrogen molecule
1 mark for describing the shape of the hydrogen molecule
1 mark for elaborating on the sigma (σ) bond in the hydrogen molecule
1 mark for elaborating on the linear shape of the hydrogen molecule
